name: Class Cohesion
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 10: Classes
problem: Classes accumulate instance variables that are only used by a subset of their methods, signalling that multiple unrelated concepts are sharing one class and should be separated into highly cohesive classes.
steps: Count how many methods use each instance variable; every variable should be used by most methods.
Identify subsets of variables and methods that cluster together and extract them into separate classes.
Maximise cohesion: each method of the resulting class should operate on most or all of its instance variables.
Applying this rule often produces many small classes, which is the desired outcome.
Use cohesion analysis as a driver for refactoring large classes into focused components.
